title: IT Support: ML Command Center

ops.note: start page identity before capture (startUrl ml-command-center.html; the cross-customer NOC board opens directly, full-width with its own header)
loaded: Micrologic NOC brand, 5 KPI tiles, 3 tier filters, 3 saved views, 10 incident rows, no agent-service error state

read-only tour proof: the page never navigates. Only the tier filters (step 4) and saved views (step 6) are ever clicked — both are verified view-only, non-navigating, non-mutating (inline JS toggles .active and re-renders locally via applyFilter(); the two page scripts contain zero fetch/XHR/POST/storage/beacon). Everything else is pointed at, never pressed.

mutation guardrail: the incident feed holds exactly 10 rows the whole lesson — proves no incident was created, opened, claimed, assigned, or resolved (the feed is static demo data unaffected by the view-only tier/view toggles)
safety guardrail: the customer detail drawer (which surfaces the parked Create Ticket / View Full Profile assign-claim controls) never opens — no incident row or map site is ever clicked into
step 1: enter from the bottom-left toward the Micrologic NOC header title as p01 says 'This is the Micrologic N-O-C'
step 1 (p01): rest just under the Micrologic NOC title (callout ①)
step 1 (p02-p05): ease wide over the whole board while the cross-customer framing is spoken ('One board, watching every customer we support')
step 1 (p05 tail): hold wide for 'this one sits above all of them, so you catch trouble before the phone even rings'
step 2 (p06): 'Look at the top strip' — settle under the header status cluster
step 2 (p07): the red 2 CRITICAL count of customers in critical shape right now
step 2 (p08): the green LIVE badge and the 'Updated just now' note
step 2 (p09): hold on 'Updated just now' — the board refreshes itself, never stale
step 2 (p10): the header search box, scoped to customers, locations, and incidents (located, never typed in)
step 3 (p11): 'Under the header, three tabs: all customers, enterprise, general' — settle under the scope filters
step 3 (p11): name Enterprise
step 3 (p11): name General
step 3 (p13-p15): hold under the filter row while 'the same instinct as the location scope' is explained — view-only, touches nothing underneath

step 4 (p16): view-only toggle. 'Tap Enterprise' is heard at 57.68; the press lands in the trailing gap (58.74–59.27) so the board is re-scoped to Enterprise (KPI Customers 8→3, map + Locations/Customers panes narrow) when p17 'Everything tightens to that tier' begins. Proven non-mutating: inline JS only toggles .active + calls applyFilter() (local re-render); no network/storage writes exist in either page script.
step 4 (p18): 'Tap All Customers and the whole network snaps back' — the restore press lands so the board is back to all 8 customers as 'snaps back' is spoken; fully reversible, one click
step 4 (p19): ease off the filters for 'It is a filter on your view, nothing deeper, and it undoes itself in a single click'
step 5 (p20): 'Beside the filters, three small counts' — settle under the status chips
step 5 (p21): Critical
step 5 (p21): warning
step 5 (p21): healthy
step 5 (p22-p24): hold under the chips — the room in one glance; when the critical number climbs, slow down and look closer
step 6 (p25): 'Now the saved views' — settle under the VIEWS presets
step 6 (p26): All Accounts is everything
step 6 (p27): Enterprise Critical
step 6 (p27-p28): Needs Attention — the preset that drags the board to what deserves your eyes first

step 6 (p29): view-only saved view. 'Reach for Needs Attention when you walk in cold and want the short list' — the press narrows the board to the critical+warning short list (KPI Customers 8→5) as the line lands. Proven non-mutating (applyFilter re-render only).
step 6 (p30): 'then drop back to All Accounts' — the restore press returns the full board (8 customers); reversible, view only, same as the filters
step 6 (p31): hold beside the presets for 'View only, same as the filters'
step 7 (p32): 'Across the top, five cards' — settle above the KPI band
step 7 (p33): name the five cards left to right — Customers
step 7 (p33): critical
step 7 (p33): warning
step 7 (p33): healthy
step 7 (p33): and average S-L-A
step 7 (p34): the first four sort your accounts by health
step 7 (p35-p36): the last one is the service level for the whole board — a number you can quote in a stand-up
step 7 (p36 tail): ease off the band
step 8 (p37): 'Read the small print under each count' — settle on the E/G split line
step 8 (p38): every card splits enterprise against general — Customers 3E · 5G
step 8 (p38): Critical 2E · 0G
step 8 (p38): Warning 1E · 2G
step 8 (p38): Healthy 0E · 3G
step 8 (p39-p41): hold on the splits — a card tells you the total and which tier carries the weight, where you decide who gets the first call
step 9 (p42): 'The average S-L-A card earns a second look' — settle above the AVG SLA card
step 9 (p43): it reads just over ninety-seven percent (97.1%)
step 9 (p44): the day-over-day drift beneath it — a down arrow is a quiet alarm (↓ 0.3% vs yesterday)
step 9 (p45-p46): attainment slipped since yesterday, worth chasing down before it grows
step 10 (p47): 'Drop to Network Coverage' — settle on the panel header
step 10 (p48): every supported site painted by health — green, amber, red (the legend)
step 10 (p49): the header counts the customers, the locations, and the states behind them
step 10 (p50-p51): ease over the map — the shape of the whole network, where a regional pattern jumps out
step 11 (p52): 'The map is live under your cursor' — ease over the map body
step 11 (p53): plus and minus zoom, the arrows go full screen — located from beside, never pressed
step 11 (p54): the numbered bubbles group nearby sites by their worst case (pointed at over the map)
step 11 (p55): 'Click a state or a cluster and it drops you into that region' — gestured over the map, never clicked (no site is opened on camera)
step 12 (p56): 'Swing to the feed on the right' — settle above the feed tabs
step 12 (p57): incidents
step 12 (p57): locations
step 12 (p57): customers, each with a live count
step 12 (p58): Incidents is the default and the one you live in — pointed at, tab is not switched
step 12 (p59-p60): the other two re-cut the same trouble by site and by account
step 13 (p61): 'The feed stacks by severity' — settle beside the Critical band header
step 13 (p62): Critical up top
step 13 (p62): then warning
step 13 (p62): then info and monitoring, each with its own tally
step 13 (p63-p64): hold beside the bands — these count incidents, while the cards up top count customers; two different denominators
step 14 (p65): 'Now read one row' — settle above the top incident row (row is a link; it is read, never opened)
step 14 (p66): the customer
step 14 (p66): its tier
step 14 (p66): the headline of what broke
step 14 (p66): a tag for the kind of failure
step 14 (p67): an owner or a status
step 14 (p67): and how long it has been running
step 14 (p68): that last part matters most — the age
step 14 (p69): 'An old, unassigned critical is the one that should bother you' — point at a live Unassigned chip (never claimed or assigned)
step 14 (p70): ease off — 'Read it here; you work it somewhere else'
step 15 (p71-p72): recap — 'So that is the cockpit. Filters and saved views aim it' — start at the filter row
step 15 (p72): and the saved views
step 15 (p73): the cards and the map give you the shape at a glance — the KPI band
step 15 (p73): sweep down into the map
step 15 (p74): the feed tells you what is broken and who has it
step 15 (p75): 'Nothing here changes a thing' — ease to center
step 15 (p76-p77): you spot it here, then open the customer, ticket, or alert somewhere else to fix it
step 15 (p78): final park, clear of content and captions, as the close lands
end state: still on the ML Command Center, unchanged
end state: feed intact — 10 incident rows; nothing was created, opened, claimed, assigned, or resolved
end state: the customer detail drawer never opened — Create Ticket / View Full Profile were never reached

map states / cluster bubbles (zoom navigation — pointed at, never clicked on camera)
